message:
Add filesystem utilities and directory discovery dependencies

Add dirs and walkdir crates to support connector implementations that
need to discover agent-specific data directories and recursively scan
log file hierarchies.

dirs crate (platform-aware directory resolution):
Provides home_dir(), config_dir(), data_local_dir() functions returning
Option<PathBuf> for platform-appropriate locations following OS conventions:
- Linux: XDG Base Directory spec (~/.config, ~/.local/share, $HOME)
- macOS: ~/Library/Application Support, ~/Library/Preferences, $HOME
- Windows: %APPDATA%, %LOCALAPPDATA%, %USERPROFILE%

Used in connector detection to locate agent data roots without hardcoding
paths:
- Cline: dirs::home_dir().join(".config/Code/User/globalStorage/...") on
  Linux vs dirs::home_dir().join("Library/Application Support/Code/...")
  on macOS
- Gemini: Fall back to dirs::home_dir().join(".gemini/tmp") when
  $GEMINI_HOME not set
- Future: Config module will use dirs::config_dir() for app config.toml
  location (e.g., ~/.config/coding-agent-search/config.toml on Linux)

Alternative considered: directories crate (more comprehensive with
ProjectDirs::from(qualifier, organization, application) for full XDG
support). Chose dirs for simplicity—only need home_dir() for now,
upgrade to directories when implementing full config module (PLAN § 11.1).

walkdir crate (recursive directory traversal):
Efficient depth-first directory walking with DirEntry iterator, filtering,
and follow_links control. Superior to std::fs::read_dir recursion:
- Single-pass iterator (no manual recursion boilerplate)
- Error handling per entry (continue on permission denied for one file
  instead of aborting entire scan)
- Performance: batches stat() calls, reuses file descriptors
- Cross-platform: handles Windows junction points, Unix symlinks

Used in connector scanning to find log files in nested directory structures:
- Codex: Scan $CODEX_HOME/sessions/YYYY/MM/DD/rollout-*.jsonl where
  YYYY/MM/DD creates ~1000 nested directories (3 years * 12 months * 30
  days), WalkDir avoids manual nested loops
- Gemini: Scan ~/.gemini/tmp/<project-hash>/*.json where project-hash
  directories are flat but numerous (hundreds of projects), WalkDir
  filters by .is_file() and extension efficiently

Example usage pattern (Codex connector):
```rust
for entry in WalkDir::new(sessions_dir).into_iter().flatten() {
    if entry.file_type().is_file()
        && entry.file_name().to_str().map(|n| n.starts_with("rollout-") && n.ends_with(".jsonl")).unwrap_or(false)
    {
        // Parse this JSONL log
    }
}
```
.flatten() discards I/O errors (e.g., permission denied on temporary
directory), avoiding panic on inaccessible paths while continuing scan.

Cargo.lock updates:
Both dirs and walkdir are pure-Rust with minimal transitive dependencies:
- dirs: 3 deps (cfg-if, libc, windows-sys) for platform detection
- walkdir: 1 dep (same-file for cycle detection in symlink traversal)
No system library dependencies (both use libc/windows-sys for syscalls),
safe for static linking in release builds.

Version constraints:
Wildcard (*) per project policy (AGENTS.md line 11) to track latest
stable releases. Current versions at commit time:
- dirs = "~5.0" (latest)
- walkdir = "~2.5" (latest)
Running cargo update periodically ensures bug fixes and performance
improvements incorporated automatically.

Why not glob/globwalk for pattern matching?
WalkDir + manual extension checks preferred over glob::glob("**/*.jsonl")
because:
- Explicit control: filter by file_type().is_file() before accessing
  file_name() avoids stat() on directories when pattern match would
  fail anyway
- Error handling: WalkDir yields Result per entry, glob yields Path and
  swallows errors, making failures silent
- Future: connectors will need metadata (mtime for incremental scans,
  size for progress estimation) requiring DirEntry.metadata() which
  WalkDir provides directly

Related: PLAN_TO_MAKE_CODING_AGENT_SESSION_SEARCH.md § 7.2 (Codex
session scanning with WalkDir), § 7.4 (Gemini tmp directory enumeration),
§ 7.6 (Cline VS Code globalStorage detection with platform paths),
§ 11.1 (future config module with directories crate), Cargo.toml
(wildcard policy).

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 crossterm = "*"
+dirs = "*"
+walkdir = "*"
